vital role family courts determine best parenting arrangements children separating couples, generally first using mediation consultation with family counsellors and, where negotiation fails, through court decision.
situation becomes very difficult once orders have been made, parent wilfully ignores them.
Judges taking more forceful approach with parents ignore court orders and, particularly with parents abscond with their children, such Brisbane mother went three years with children subsequently jailed months.
children's grandmother also given custodial sentence will spend least three months gaol.
Judge Michael Jarrett berated children's mother "flagrant disregard" custody arrangement made between ex-husband.
May, Police arrested another North Queensland woman after abducted grandson from primary school Port Macquarie.
After found, police returned them Queensland where grandmother faced court proceedings child abduction.
Where parent involved family proceedings removes child from their usual residence prevents other parent from having court-ordered contact with child because they cannot located, other parent apply Court seeking recovery order.
recovery order fails, parent apply court publication order which allows photographs names missing child published media.
Often publication results children being located through public information.
child abducted overseas country, different process applies.
Australia highest capita rates world parents unlawfully kidnapping their children fleeing with them overseas.
Last year alone, there were ongoing Australian applications child abduction Hague Convention signatories.
child taken country that member Hague Convention, steps taken through international agreement mediate custody battles.
However, legal resolution becomes infinitely more difficult child taken non-Hague Convention countries like India, China Lebanon.
This article written Margaret Miller, partner Bell Legal Group general information only.
legal advice intended should relied upon such.
